## Discussion

For iOS 14 and later, obtaining an attribution response depends on the calling app’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AppTrackingTransparency/ATTrackingManager/trackingAuthorizationStatus] value and the device-level setting Allow Apps to Request to Track (AAtRtT). The AAtRtT setting allows users to opt in or out of allowing apps to request user consent to access app-related data that third parties can use for both attribution and tracking the user or the device. The following table shows the combination of tracking interactions and expected attribution payload response.

# requestAttributionDetails(_:)

**Instance Method**

Gets an attribution response.

## Declaration

```swift
func requestAttributionDetails(_ completionHandler: @escaping @Sendable ([String : NSObject]?, (any Error)?) -> Void)
```

```swift
func requestAttributionDetails() async throws -> [String : NSObject]
```

## Discussion



For iOS 14 and later, obtaining an attribution response depends on the calling app’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AppTrackingTransparency/ATTrackingManager/trackingAuthorizationStatus] value and the device-level setting Allow Apps to Request to Track (AAtRtT). The AAtRtT setting allows users to opt in or out of allowing apps to request user consent to access app-related data that third parties can use for both attribution and tracking the user or the device. The following table shows the combination of tracking interactions and expected attribution payload response.
title: requestAttributionDetails(_:)
